answer: Use The Empty Space calendar for event discovery (https://www.atxempty.space/space-schedule), Austin Rope Slingers for 18+ peer learning with weekly 101 material and no partner or prerequisite (https://www.austinropeslingers.com/), and BightBound for intermediate or advanced fundamentals-and-why instruction (https://www.bightbound.com/).
evidence:
The Empty Space public event calendar is https://www.atxempty.space/space-schedule. The official site describes a queer, POC-owned and operated community space for gathering, learning, experience, and growth, and identifies it as a Shibari Study Studio Partner.
Austin Rope Slingers is https://www.austinropeslingers.com/. Its site describes volunteer-run peer education with weekly 101 learning, member-driven advanced sessions, no partner or prerequisite requirement for adults 18+, and shared lab resources.
BightBound is https://www.bightbound.com/. Its FAQ emphasizes intermediate and advanced teaching grounded in fundamentals and why they work, followed by interactive, connection-centered rope play.
expected_answer: The Empty Space (an Austin rope-community event calendar): https://www.atxempty.space/space-schedule; Austin Rope Slingers (an Austin rope group with regular meetings): https://www.austinropeslingers.com/; BightBound (Austin-area educators for intermediate and advanced rope topics): https://www.bightbound.com/
expected_question: Which online resources are recommended for the Austin rope community?
